title: Call abstract class' ctor from a non-abstract class' ctor
body:
Hello!

The following code does not compile:

```systemverilog
virtual class foo;
    function new(); endfunction
endclass

class bar extends foo;
    function new();
        super.new();
    endfunction
endclass
```

The error message:
> Illegal to call 'new' using an abstract virtual class (IEEE 1800-2017 8.21)

The referenced chapter explicitly allows to call a constructor of the abstract class if the call is initiated in an non-abstract class constructor:
> An object of an abstract class shall not be constructed directly. Its constructor may only be called indirectly through the chaining of constructor calls originating in an extended non-abstract object.

Version: Verilator 5.004 2022-12-14 rev UNKNOWN.REV
OS: macOS Ventura 13.1
